vulnerability.reference: Localization teams lack tools to verify speaker identity consistency across edited audio segments.
vulnerability.generated: Dubbing workflows rely on zero-shot editing without safeguards to prevent unintended speaker identity or prosody drift during automated manipulation.

impact.reference: Speaker identity shifts cause viewer cognitive dissonance, reducing immersion and content enjoyment.
impact.generated: Lip-sync mismatches between audio and visual elements degrade viewer immersion and dubbing quality in multilingual content.

dose_response.reference: Zero-shot editing shifts speaker identity/prosody, exposing dubbing teams and audiences to mismatches, causing cognitive dissonance and reinforcing low-quality stereotypes.
dose_response.generated: Unsupervised semantic manipulation of audio signals via frozen diffusion model latent spaces, through dubbing artists using diffusion-based tools, triggers reliance on zero-shot editing without safeguards against speaker identity or prosody drift, leading to lip-sync mismatches that degrade viewer immersion and dubbing quality.
